interpretation: The curve is broadly upward-sloping but exhibits a hump, with the 2Y yield (11.95%) exceeding the 5Y (10.82%) and 10Y (11.15%), while the 15Y (12.68%) peaks highest. The 2Y anomaly likely reflects tight banking sector liquidity and aggressive re-pricing around BB's 10% repo rate amid persistent inflation (8-10% range). The 364D dip below 182D suggests near-term rate cut expectations. The steep long end reflects fiscal deficit concerns and inflation risk premium in a shallow government bond market.
implications: The 2Y bulge signals banks are demanding premium for medium-term liquidity lock-up amid CRR constraints and advance-deposit ratio pressures
BB's 250bps repo-SDF corridor and elevated short-end yields suggest monetary tightening is biting but inflation expectations remain unanchored beyond 2 years
The steep 10Y-20Y segment (50bps) indicates growing term premium driven by fiscal financing concerns and limited institutional demand depth for ultra-long tenors
